data.title: American Eagle Outfitters declares $0.125 dividend
data.description:

  • Forward yield 4.87%

data.title: Costco reports core comparable sales rose 6.0% in May in a slight deceleration
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) reported comparable sales for the retail month of May rose 4.3%, consisting of a 4.1% gain in the U.S., a 3.3% increase in Canada, and a 6.3% rise for the international business.

E-commerce sales increased 11.6% during the month. Food and sundries comparable sales were up mid-to-high single digits for the month, and fresh food comparables sales were up at a high single-digit rate. Ancillary sales were down during the month.

After stripping out the impact of F/X and gas prices, comparable sales were up 6.0% in May, including a 5.5% increase in the U.S. Both marks were lower than the pace seen in April.

Total Costco May 2025 sales were up 6.8% to $20.97 billion.

Costco currently operates 905 warehouses, including 624 in the United States and Puerto Rico, 109 in Canada, 41 in Mexico, 37 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 19 in Korea, 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, seven in China, five in Spain, two in France, and one each in Iceland, New Zealand, and Sweden. Costco also operates e-commerce sites in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Mexico, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, and Australia.

Shares of Costco (NASDAQ:COST) slipped 0.6% in postmarket action.

data.title: Five Below posts results beat, CFO exits for personal reasons
data.description:
Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) on Wednesday reported a top and bottom line beat for the first quarter, updated its forecast, and announced the departure of its chief financial officer.

Shares of the company were down 3.5% in immediate reaction to the report but turned positive and are now up 3.5% in post-market trading.

In Q1, comparable sales rose 7.1%, ahead of the consensus estimate of 6.4%, driven by broad-based strength across the majority of its merchandising worlds. It opened 55 new stores and had a total of 1,826 stores in 44 states, up 13.8% from last year.

Net income for the quarter was $41.1M, or $0.75 per share, compared with $31.5M, or $0.57 per share, for the same period last year.

Excluding items, the company earned 86 cents apiece, beating the average analyst estimate by 3 cents.

Revenue rose 19.5% to $970.5M and was ahead of the consensus mark by over $4M. 

For the second quarter, the company guided net sales to be in the range of $975M to $995M, which is ahead of the consensus estimate of $958.3M. Adjusted earnings per share are expected to be around $0.50 and $0.62 (midpoint $0.56, estimate $0.58). The forecast includes the impact of tariffs.

Five Below expects about a 7% to 9% rise in comparable sales and anticipates opening about 30 net new stores in the current quarter.

"Looking ahead, this unwavering focus on the core customer combined with disciplined execution of our strategy and the agility of our teams position us to deliver our financial and operational objectives as we navigate the impact of tariffs and the associated uncertainty in the current global trade environment," CEO Winnie Park said in a statement.

Citing personal reasons, the specialty discount store chain's current CFO, Kristy Chipman, is stepping down, the company said. COO Ken Bull, who was previously the finance chief for more than ten years, will assume her duties on an interim basis.

data.title: Rivian prices $1.25B green notes offering to refinance 2026 notes
data.description:
• Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) and affiliated issuers have priced $1.25B in 10.000% senior secured green notes due 2031.
  • Proceeds will be used, along with cash on hand, to redeem all outstanding $1.25B floating rate senior secured notes due 2026.
  • The note offering is expected to close on June 12, 2025.
  • The structure anticipates future funding of Rivian’s Department of Energy loan facility, impacting lien priorities.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Colliers to acquire controlling interest in Astris, terms undisclosed
data.description:
• Colliers (NASDAQ:CIGI) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a controlling interest in Astris Infrastructure, a global investment banking firm specializing in infrastructure and energy transition.
  • Under Colliers’ unique partnership model, the senior leadership of Astris Finance, along with nine senior team members who will become partners, will retain significant ownership in the business.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025. Terms were not disclosed.
  • “With Astris Finance, we significantly expand our investment banking capabilities, allowing us to better serve institutional clients and capitalize on the growing global demand for infrastructure that supports urbanization, energy security, and decarbonization.” said Elias Mulamoottil, Co-CIO of Colliers.

data.title: Cboe multiply-listed options ADV slides 4% M/M, gains 28% Y/Y in May
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) multiply-listed options average daily volume ("ADV") of 12.7M contracts slid 4.1% from a month earlier, but climbed 27.6% from the year-ago period, the derivatives exchange said on Wednesday.

Index options ADV totaled 4.33M contracts last month, down 14.9% from April and up 14.7% from May 2024.

Futures contracts ADV dropped 46.4% sequentially and 22.5% Y/Y to 166K contracts.

U.S. equities - on exchange - ADV was 1.86B matched shares, down from 2.12B in April and up from 1.42B a year before.

Canadian equities ADV of 135.1M matched shares compared with 170.5M in April and 140.2M in last year's May.

European equities ADV stood at €12.1B vs. €17.4B in April and €8.63B in May 2024.

data.title: Canadian Natural Resources restarts oil sands operation as wildfire danger eases
data.description:
Canadian Natural Resources (NYSE:CNQ) said late Tuesday it restarted its Jackfish 1 oil sands site in northern Alberta after a second day of rain slowed the spread of wildfires.

Canada's biggest oil producer said its operation at the site will ramp up over the next several days, targeting full production of ~36,500 bbl/day by Friday, after it had evacuated non-essential workers and halted production as a precaution last weekend due to wildfires burning south of Fort McMurray.

Conditions have since improved from conditions last week that forced evacuations and shut as much as 7% of Canada's daily oil production.

Still, Bloomberg reported that ~462K bbl/day of oil production is within 10 km of a total of 25 wildfires that burn out of control in oil-producing Alberta province.

As of Wednesday morning, 238K bbl/day of production remained halted at Christina Lake oil sands facilities operated by Cenovus Energy (CVE) and MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF), although there have been no reports of significant damage to oil infrastructure or company assets due to the fires.

data.title: Cullinan Therapeutics in pact with Genrix Bio for velinotamig
data.description:
Cullinan Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CGEM) signed a deal with Genrix Bio for an exclusive license for velinotamig, a bispecific T cell engager. 

As per the deal, Cullinan will pay Genrix Bio a $20M upfront license fee for exclusive rights to develop and commercialize velinotamig in all disease areas worldwide other than Greater China. 

Genrix can earn up to $292M in development and regulatory milestones and an extra $400M based on sales, alongside tiered royalties ranging from the mid-single digits to the mid-teens on potential net sales outside Greater China.

Genrix aims to start an early stage trial in China by year-end for patients with autoimmune conditions.Cullinan plans to use this data to speed up the program's clinical development.

Cullinan also confirmed it has enough cash to operate until 2028.
